topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The slip of paper felt like sandpaper against my palm. It was tucked deep inside a seldom-used drawer of Mark’s desk, a place I’d never dared to pry into. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. The numbers staring back at me—a substantial sum, hidden away—made my stomach clench with a sudden, sickening wave of realization. I picked up the note, my fingers trembling so badly I had to steady them on the cool wood of the desk. Looking up at Mark, who was engrossed in a work email, I saw a flicker of something—surprise, perhaps, or maybe just mild annoyance—and instantly, I wanted to disappear. I slowly folded the paper, my gaze fixed on the floor, the silence in the room suddenly deafening, filled only by the frantic drumming inside my chest.

[story 2]

I found the ledger tucked beneath a stack of old tax documents. The ink was faded, but the figures were unmistakable: a significant amount of money, meticulously saved, completely unknown to me. A cold sweat broke out on my forehead, prickling the skin just above my hairline. Every instinct screamed at me to shove the book back into its hiding spot, to pretend I had never seen it. When I finally looked over at Sarah, who was humming softly in the kitchen, I felt a profound awkwardness wash over me. I busied myself rearranging the papers on the shelf with unnecessary vigor, my movements jerky and too fast. My voice, when I finally spoke, came out thin and reedy, a stark contrast to the heavy, secret weight settling squarely on my shoulders.

[story 3]

It was a misplaced envelope, slipped into the lining of an old winter coat. Inside, a bank statement, clearly belonging to Mark, detailed an account I hadn't known existed. My breath hitched in my throat. The sheer scale of it hit me like a physical blow. I stood frozen in the hallway, the fluorescent light buzzing overhead seeming unbearably bright. I could feel the heat rising in my cheeks, a flush that spread rapidly down my neck. I quickly smoothed the paper flat, trying to smooth out the disarray in my hands, desperate to conceal the evidence of this hidden life, this secret hoard, before anyone could possibly notice the tremor running through my entire frame.
